title: ConflictScore: Identifying and Measuring How Language Models Handle Conflicting Evidence
abstract: arXiv:2606.26437v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Existing metrics for factuality and faithfulness evaluate whether an answer is supported or contradicted by its grounding documents, but they fail to capture when both supporting and contradicting evidence coexist. We introduce ConflictScore, a novel metric that quantifies how well a model's response acknowledges conflicting evidence in its grounding documents. Our framework decomposes responses into atomic claims, labels each claim against each grounding document, and then aggregates these labels into two complementary measures: ConflictScore-Count (CS-C), the proportion of claims exhibiting conflicts, and ConflictScore-Ratio (CS-R), the balance between supporting and contradicting evidence. We develop ConflictBench, a benchmark covering diverse forms of conflicts such as ambiguity, contradiction, and divergent opinions, to systematically evaluate our metric. Experiments show that ConflictScore effectively detects overconfident claims

Title: ConflictScore: Identifying and Measuring How Language Models Handle Conflicting Evidence
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26437v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Existing metrics for factuality and faithfulness evaluate whether an answer is supported or contradicted by its grounding documents, but they fail to capture when both supporting and contradicting evidence coexist. We introduce ConflictScore, a novel metric that quantifies how well a model's response acknowledges conflicting evidence in its grounding documents. Our framework decomposes responses into atomic claims, labels each claim against each grounding document, and then aggregates these labels into two complementary measures: ConflictScore-Count (CS-C), the proportion of claims exhibiting conflicts, and ConflictScore-Ratio (CS-R), the balance between supporting and contradicting evidence. We develop ConflictBench, a benchmark covering diverse forms of conflicts such as ambiguity, contradiction, and divergent opinions, to systematically evaluate our metric. Experiments show that ConflictScore effectively detects overconfident claims
translated_title: ConflictScore：识别与衡量语言模型如何处理冲突证据
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26437v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：现有的事实性和忠实性度量指标可以评估一个答案是否被其基础文档支持或反驳，但它们无法捕捉到支持和反驳证据同时存在的情况。我们引入了ConflictScore，这是一种新颖的度量指标，用于量化模型在其基础文档中承认冲突证据的程度。我们的框架将答案分解为原子声明，对每个声明针对每个基础文档进行标注，然后将这些标注聚合为两个互补的度量指标：ConflictScore-Count（CS-C），即表现出冲突的声明比例；以及ConflictScore-Ratio（CS-R），即支持与反驳证据之间的平衡程度。我们开发了ConflictBench，这是一个涵盖歧义、矛盾、分歧观点等多种冲突形式的基准数据集，以系统性地评估我们的度量指标。实验表明，ConflictScore能够有效检测过于自信的声明。
tldr: ConflictScore通过将答案分解为原子声明并分别标注其与各基础文档的关系，再聚合为冲突比例和证据平衡度两个互补指标，从而有效量化语言模型在存在冲突证据时的回答质量。
en_tdlr: ConflictScore effectively quantifies the quality of language model responses in the presence of conflicting evidence by decomposing answers into atomic claims, labeling each claim against each grounding document, and aggregating these into two complementary metrics: conflict proportion and evidence balance.
